preamble: Denasalization with raising and rounding of the nasalized open front vowel [ã] to the close-mid back rounded vowel [o]. This shift involves loss of nasality combined with raising of vowel height and backspace movement with rounding, occurring through denasalization and vowel compensatory changes in Romance language evolution.

examples.note: Denasalization with vowel raising: Old French [ã] → Modern French [ɔ̃] or [o] in certain contexts
Historical shift from nasalized low vowel to raised nasalized mid vowel, with some instances of complete denasalization
Denasalization with raising: nasalized open vowel shifts toward close-mid position, characteristic of some Portuguese dialects
Northern Portuguese and some Galician dialects show denasalization with simultaneous vowel raising before consonants

related.label: Denasalization with raising to mid vowel
